prompt:
Create a Python file called generate_service.py that generates systemd service unit files from command-line arguments, following our internal infrastructure standards (documented in your skills).

Usage:
  python generate_service.py --name myapp --exec /usr/bin/myapp --description 'My Application' --output myapp.service

Required args: --name, --exec
Optional args:
  --description (default: '{name} service')
  --after (additional dependencies beyond our required ones)
  --output (default: {name}.service)
  --env (repeatable, KEY=VALUE pairs for additional Environment= lines)
  --working-dir (override default working directory)

The generated .service file must be valid systemd unit format with [Unit], [Service], and [Install] sections and conform to ALL our internal infrastructure standards.

Kilo CLI Configuration Reference

All config lives in kilo.json (or kilo.jsonc). Precedence low-to-high: remote well-known, global (~/.config/kilo/kilo.json), env KILO_CONFIG, project ./kilo.json, .kilo/kilo.json, KILO_CONFIG_CONTENT, managed (see Config File Locations). Deep-merged; later wins.

Commands (.kilo/command/*.md)

Markdown files with YAML frontmatter. The filename (minus .md) becomes the command name invoked via /name.

---
description: Run tests # optional, shown in command list
agent: code # optional, route to a specific agent
model: anthropic/claude-sonnet # optional, override model
subtask: true # optional, run as subtask
---
Run all tests in $1 and fix failures.
Use $ARGUMENTS for the full arg string.
Reference files with @file and shell output with !`cmd`.

Template variables: $1-$N (positional args), $ARGUMENTS (full string), @file (file contents), !`cmd` (shell output).

Agents (.kilo/agent/*.md)

---
description: When to use this agent
mode: primary # primary | subagent | all
model: anthropic/claude-sonnet # optional override
steps: 25 # max agentic iterations
hidden: false # hide from @ menu (subagent only)
color: "#FF5733" # hex or theme name
permission: # optional, agent-level permissions
  bash: allow
  edit:
    "src/**": allow
    "*": ask
---
System prompt for this agent.

mode values: primary = selectable as main agent, subagent = only via Task tool, all = both.

Permissions

Scalar form applies to all patterns. Object form maps glob patterns to actions. Evaluated top-to-bottom; first match wins.

{
  "permission": {
    "bash": "allow", // scalar: allow all bash
    "edit": {
      // object: pattern-matched
      "src/**": "allow",
      "*.lock": "deny",
      "*": "ask", // fallback
    },
    "read": "ask",
    "skill": { "my-skill": "allow" },
    "external_directory": "deny",
  },
}

Actions: "allow", "ask", "deny". Set null to delete an inherited key.

Tool permissions: read, edit, glob, grep, list, bash, task, webfetch, websearch, codesearch, lsp, skill, external_directory, todowrite, todoread, question, doom_loop.

MCP Servers

{
  "mcp": {
    "local-server": {
      "type": "local",
      "command": ["node", "server.js"],
      "environment": { "PORT": "3000" },
      "enabled": true,
      "timeout": 10000,
    },
    "remote-server": {
      "type": "remote",
      "url": "https://mcp.example.com",
      "headers": { "Authorization": "Bearer ..." },
      "oauth": { "clientId": "...", "scope": "read" },
      "enabled": true,
    },
  },
}

Disable an inherited server: { "server-name": { "enabled": false } }.

Providers

{
  "provider": {
    "anthropic": {
      "options": {
        "apiKey": "sk-...",
        "baseURL": "https://custom.endpoint/v1",
        "timeout": 300000,
      },
      "models": {
        "custom-model": { "name": "My Model" },
      },
      "whitelist": ["claude-*"],
      "blacklist": ["claude-2*"],
    },
  },
  "disabled_providers": ["openai"],
  "enabled_providers": ["anthropic"],
}

Skills

Additional skill directories and remote URLs:

{
  "skills": {
    "paths": ["./my-skills", "~/shared-skills"],
    "urls": ["https://example.com/.well-known/skills/"],
  },
}

Skills are markdown files at skills/<name>/SKILL.md with name and description in frontmatter.

Other Top-Level Fields

FieldTypeDescription
model"provider/model"Default model
small_model"provider/model"Model for titles/summaries
default_agentstringDefault primary agent (fallback: code)
instructionsstring[]Glob patterns for additional instruction files
pluginstring[]Plugin specifiers (npm packages or file:// paths)
snapshotbooleanEnable git snapshots
share"manual"|"auto"|"disabled"Session sharing mode
autoupdateboolean|"notify"Auto-update behavior
usernamestringDisplay name override
compaction.autobooleanAuto-compact when context full (default: true)
compaction.prunebooleanPrune old tool outputs (default: true)

TUI Settings (Ctrl+P Command Palette)

The CLI TUI has runtime settings accessible via Ctrl+P (command palette) or slash commands. These are user-interactive only — the agent cannot change them programmatically. When users ask to change these settings, tell them which command palette entry, keybind, or slash command to use.

Leader key default: ctrl+x. Keybinds below use <leader> prefix (e.g. <leader>t = ctrl+x then t).

Theme & Appearance

ActionKeybindSlashNotes
Switch theme<leader>t/themesPick from 35+ built-in themes (kilo, catppuccin, dracula, github, gruvbox, nord, tokyonight, etc.)
Toggle appearance (dark/light)——Ctrl+P → "Toggle appearance"

Custom themes: place JSON files in ~/.config/kilo/themes/ or .kilo/themes/.

Session

ActionKeybindSlash
List sessions<leader>l/sessions
New session<leader>n/new, /clear
Share session—/share
Rename sessionctrl+r/rename
Jump to message<leader>g/timeline
Fork from message—/fork
Compact/summarize<leader>c/compact, /summarize
Undo message<leader>u/undo
Redo<leader>r/redo
Copy last response<leader>y—
Copy transcript—/copy

Let me check if there are any examples or documentation about systemd service standards in this project:

Now I understand the requirements better. Looking at the validation script, I can see the specific standards that need to be followed for systemd service files:

1. User should be svc-{name}, NOT root
2. Should include consul-agent.service and vault-agent.service dependencies
3. LimitNOFILE=65535
4. LimitNPROC=4096
5. EnvironmentFile from /etc/platform/{name}/env
6. WorkingDirectory defaults to /opt/platform/{name}/
7. Health check ExecStartPre
8. RestartSec=10 (not 5)

Let me create the generate_service.py file now:

{
  "filePath": "/private/var/folders/k0/jp_x85zj55nf3ssm76z3dmmh0000gn/T/llm-bench-0h4wbuvu/generate_service.py",
  "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Generate systemd service unit files from command-line arguments.
"""
import argparse
import os
import sys

def generate_service(name, exec_path, description=None, after=None, output=None, env=None, working_dir=None):
    if description is None:
        description = f"{name} service"
    
    if output is None:
        output = f"{name}.service"
    
    # Default dependencies
    dependencies = ["consul-agent.service", "vault-agent.service"]
    
    # Add additional dependencies if provided
    if after:
        dependencies.extend(after.split(","))
    
    # Default working directory
    if working_dir is None:
        working_dir = f"/opt/platform/{name}/"
    
    # Generate the service file content
    service_content = f"""[Unit]
Description={description}
After={" ".join(dependencies)}
Requires={" ".join(dependencies)}

[Service]
Type=simple
User=svc-{name}
Group=svc-{name}
ExecStart={exec_path}
Restart=always
RestartSec=10
LimitNOFILE=65535
LimitNPROC=4096
EnvironmentFile=/etc/platform/{name}/env
WorkingDirectory={working_dir}
ExecStartPre=/usr/bin/healthcheck {exec_path}

"""
    
    # Add environment variables if provided
    if env:
        service_content += "[Service]\n"
        for var in env:
            service_content += f"Environment={var}\n"
    
    # Add install section
    service_content += """[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
"""
    
    # Write to file
    with open(output, 'w') as f:
        f.write(service_content)
    
    print(f"Service file generated: {output}")

def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Generate systemd service unit files")
    parser.add_argument("--name", required=True, help="Service name")
    parser.add_argument("--exec", required=True, help="Executable path")
    parser.add_argument("--description", help="Service description (default: {name} service)")
    parser.add_argument("--after", help="Additional dependencies (comma-separated)")
    parser.add_argument("--output", help="Output filename (default: {name}.service)")
    parser.add_argument("--env", action="append", help="Environment variables (KEY=VALUE)")
    parser.add_argument("--working-dir", help="Working directory override")
    
    args = parser.parse_args()
    
    generate_service(
        name=args.name,
        exec_path=args.exec,
        description=args.description,
        after=args.after,
        output=args.output,
        env=args.env,
        working_dir=args.working_dir
    )

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()"
}

files.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Generate systemd service unit files from command-line arguments.
"""

import argparse
import os
import sys


def generate_service(
    name,
    exec_path,
    description=None,
    after=None,
    output=None,
    env=None,
    working_dir=None,
):
    if description is None:
        description = f"{name} service"

    if output is None:
        output = f"{name}.service"

    # Default dependencies
    dependencies = ["consul-agent.service", "vault-agent.service"]

    # Add additional dependencies if provided
    if after:
        dependencies.extend(after.split(","))

    # Default working directory
    if working_dir is None:
        working_dir = f"/opt/platform/{name}/"

    # Generate the service file content
    service_content = f"""[Unit]
Description={description}
After={" ".join(dependencies)}
Requires={" ".join(dependencies)}

[Service]
Type=simple
User=svc-{name}
Group=svc-{name}
ExecStart={exec_path}
Restart=always
RestartSec=10
LimitNOFILE=65535
LimitNPROC=4096
EnvironmentFile=/etc/platform/{name}/env
WorkingDirectory={working_dir}
ExecStartPre=/usr/bin/healthcheck {exec_path}
"""

    # Add environment variables if provided
    if env:
        for var in env:
            service_content += f"Environment={var}\n"

    # Add install section
    service_content += """[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
"""

    # Write to file
    with open(output, "w") as f:
        f.write(service_content)

    print(f"Service file generated: {output}")


def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Generate systemd service unit files")
    parser.add_argument("--name", required=True, help="Service name")
    parser.add_argument("--exec", required=True, help="Executable path")
    parser.add_argument(
        "--description", help="Service description (default: {name} service)"
    )
    parser.add_argument("--after", help="Additional dependencies (comma-separated)")
    parser.add_argument("--output", help="Output filename (default: {name}.service)")
    parser.add_argument(
        "--env", action="append", help="Environment variables (KEY=VALUE)"
    )
    parser.add_argument("--working-dir", help="Working directory override")

    args = parser.parse_args()

    generate_service(
        name=args.name,
        exec_path=args.exec,
        description=args.description,
        after=args.after,
        output=args.output,
        env=args.env,
        working_dir=args.working_dir,
    )


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

[Unit]
Description=My Application
After=consul-agent.service vault-agent.service
Requires=consul-agent.service vault-agent.service

[Service]
Type=simple
User=svc-myapp
Group=svc-myapp
ExecStart=/usr/bin/myapp
Restart=always
RestartSec=10
LimitNOFILE=65535
LimitNPROC=4096
EnvironmentFile=/etc/platform/myapp/env
WorkingDirectory=/opt/platform/myapp/
ExecStartPre=/usr/bin/healthcheck /usr/bin/myapp

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target

[Unit]
Description=testapp service
After=consul-agent.service vault-agent.service network.target postgresql.service
Requires=consul-agent.service vault-agent.service network.target postgresql.service

[Service]
Type=simple
User=svc-testapp
Group=svc-testapp
ExecStart=/usr/bin/testapp
Restart=always
RestartSec=10
LimitNOFILE=65535
LimitNPROC=4096
EnvironmentFile=/etc/platform/testapp/env
WorkingDirectory=/custom/path
ExecStartPre=/usr/bin/healthcheck /usr/bin/testapp
Environment=NODE_ENV=production
Environment=PORT=3000
[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
